title: [Bug] qwen3.6 mtp crash in sglang==0.5.13
why: The issue title references MTP (multi-token prediction) in the context of a speculative decoding crash; however, reviewing the evidence more carefully, 'mtp' here refers to multi-token prediction which is a deepseek architectural primitive — but the model is Qwen3.6, not DeepSeek. Let me reassess: the issue is about Qwen3.6 MTP, not DeepSeek MTP specifically. Hard rule 5 requires at least one quote containing 'MTP', 'multi-token prediction', 'DeepSeek', 'MLA', 'multi-head latent', or 'shared expert'. The title contains 'mtp' which maps to 'MTP' (multi-token prediction), a deepseek architectural primitive that qualifies even when DeepSeek isn't named.

title: [Bug]: vllm 0.23.0 2*h20-141G mp+dep and mp +tep deploy dsv4p error
why: This issue is about deploying DeepSeek-V4-Pro (dsv4p) with configuration mismatch and weight partitioning errors, directly naming DeepSeek-V4-Pro.

title: [Bug]: DSV4-Flash MooncakeStoreConnector save never triggers when MTP speculative decoding is enabled
why: This issue explicitly involves DeepSeek-V4-Flash with MTP (multi-token prediction) speculative decoding causing a KV cache save failure, directly touching DeepSeek architectural primitives (MTP).
evidence_quotes: When deploying DeepSeek-V4-Flash with `MooncakeStoreConnector` + MTP speculative decoding (`speculative_config.method='mtp'`)
